items.headline: Major commercial project approved on West San Carlos Street
items.summary: A $696,570 commercial/industrial development is underway at 1300 W San Carlos St, representing significant economic activity in San José's commercial corridor. This is the only non-residential project in the recent permits and could bring new business or employment to the area.

items.headline: Stadium Neighborhood Relations committee continues addressing stadium impacts
items.summary: An ad hoc subcommittee focused on stadium neighborhood relations is meeting to discuss ongoing issues between Levi's Stadium operations and surrounding residents. This suggests active management of complaints or concerns from the South Bay area near the stadium.

items.headline: Cupertino launches business license amnesty and retail incentive programs
items.summary: The city council approved outreach efforts for a business license amnesty program targeting shopping centers, along with new economic development incentives for retail stores, small businesses, and startups. This could help previously unlicensed businesses get compliant while attracting new commercial activity to Cupertino.

items.headline: Great American Litter Pick-up approved as annual city event
items.summary: City Council approved the Great American Litter Pick-up as an official annual city-sponsored special event and authorized spending city funds and accepting donated materials and services. This establishes ongoing community cleanup efforts.

items.headline: City discusses federal grant funding for housing and services
items.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission reviewed Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) funding applications on March 25. These federal funds support affordable housing and community development projects that directly benefit local residents.

items.headline: Planning Commission to study San Antonio Road area transportation and land use
items.summary: The commission will examine alternatives for how San Antonio Road and its surroundings should develop, including transportation options and zoning. This study could affect traffic patterns and future development in that corridor.

items.headline: Palo Alto amending zoning code to support retail vitality downtown
items.summary: The commission is considering changes to building and zoning regulations aimed at implementing retail policies from the city's Comprehensive Plan. The changes are designed to strengthen and revitalize retail areas in Palo Alto.

items.headline: New electrical transmission lines coming to wastewater facility area
items.summary: LS Power Grid California will install electrical transmission infrastructure across the San José-Santa Clara Regional Wastewater Facility. This infrastructure project may affect the facility's operations or nearby areas.
